growth_records.medium: fermentation medium (succinic acid production) with Mg2(OH)2CO3 neutralizer/CO2 co-substrate

up.units: not biomass-normalized; consumed (glucose conversion yield 0.78 g succinate/g glucose reported)
up.method: glucose assay kit; conversion analyzed across systems

sec.method: HPLC; PEC-biohybrid at -0.3 V vs RHE, 24 h

sec.units: ~144 umol after 8 h (abiotic HER on photocathode, Faradaic eff. 48.3%)
~425.7 nmol after 3 h (abiotic CO2RR, Faradaic eff. 0.38%)

growth_records.cite: Feng, Tianhang et al. Photoelectrocatalytic-microbial biohybrid for succinic acid synthesis. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-69962-4. [PMC13039688]
growth_records.snippet: The NiO@PAA@NHS/Au@A. succinogenes biohybrid system demonstrated superior performance, achieving a remarkable 67% CO2 conversion rate along with a glucose conversion yield of 0.78 g/g glucose... the PEC-biohybrid demonstrated the highest yi
growth_records.notes: NON-STANDARD growth condition: this is a photoelectrocatalytic-microbial biohybrid (electrode-immobilized cells, external electrons, CO2 co-substrate), not a conventional planktonic fermentation. Succinate production rate 1.41 g/L/h/cm2 is normalized to electrode area, NOT biomass (no gDW / OD / growth rate reported) -> cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Product yield = 0.78 g succinic acid / g glucose (biohybrid). Succinic acid = 57.18% of total product (Fig 3f). Byproducts acetic/formic/pyruvic acid detected but not quantified in text. Comparators: EC-biohybrid 0.75, PC-biohybrid 0.58, dark/open-circuit 0.21 g/L/h/cm2. Cells (WT and Au@A.succinogenes) resuspended to OD600 ~2.0 for immobilization. Strain 130Z ATCC 55618. Fermentation at 37 C, anaerobic, CO2-saturated.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A growth/salts + B phosphate + C carbohydrate: 60 g/L xylose)

up.method: derived = D x (effective feed x sugar conversion) = 0.05 x (57.0 x 0.80); volumetric

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor: specific growth rate decoupled from dilution rate due to cell retention; system near non-growth/maintenance mode, biomass negligible — DCW ~0.19 g/L reported for D=0.05 from an earlier study, cited)
growth_records.snippet: Steady states were achieved at dilution rates (D) of 0.05 and 0.10 h-1 (Table 1) with succinic acid as the major product... D=0.05: productivity 1.54, SA 32.5 g/L, yield on xylose 0.72, SA/AA 4.2, FA/AA 0.36, sugar conversion 80%, effective
growth_records.notes: Table 1 clean-xylose baseline, D=0.05 h-1. Measured: SA productivity 1.54 g/L/h, SA titre 32.5 g/L, yield 0.72 g/g xylose, SA/AA 4.2 g/g, FA/AA 0.36 g/g, xylose conversion 80%, effective xylose feed 57.0 g/L. Derived xylose uptake rate = 0.05 x 57.0 x 0.80 = 2.28 g/L/h = 15.2 mmol/L/h (xylose MW 150.13). AA productivity = 1.54/4.2 = 0.367 g/L/h; FA productivity = 0.367 x 0.36 = 0.132 g/L/h. Rates are volumetric (g or mmol per L per h): biomass DCW not measured here (excluded from mass balance), so gDW-specific rates not derivable. NOTE: immobilised biofilm reactor -> mu is NOT equal to D.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A+B) with 60 g/L xylose

up.method: derived = D x (effective feed x conversion) = 0.10 x (56.7 x 0.60); volumetric

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor; mu decoupled from D, near maintenance mode)
growth_records.snippet: D=0.10: productivity 2.64, SA 26.4 g/L, yield 0.77, SA/AA 4.2, FA/AA 0.37, sugar conversion 60%, effective xylose feed 56.7 g/L (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 clean-xylose baseline, D=0.10 h-1. Measured: SA productivity 2.64 g/L/h, SA titre 26.4 g/L, yield 0.77 g/g, SA/AA 4.2, FA/AA 0.37, xylose conversion 60%, feed 56.7 g/L. Derived xylose uptake = 0.10 x 56.7 x 0.60 = 3.40 g/L/h = 22.7 mmol/L/h. AA productivity = 2.64/4.2 = 0.629 g/L/h; FA = 0.629 x 0.37 = 0.233 g/L/h. Volumetric only (no biomass measured).

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A+B) with deacetylated dilute-acid-pretreated corn stover hydrolysate (DDAP-H), 70 g/L total sugars

up.method: derived = D x (total feed sugars x total conversion) = 0.02 x (69.9 x 0.813)

sec.method: derived qSA = D x CSA = 0.02 x 39.6
reported CFA remained near/equal to zero

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor; mu decoupled from D)
growth_records.snippet: DDAP-H fermentations performed in duplicate at dilution rates of 0.02, 0.03 and 0.04 h-1... CSA remained fairly constant at between 38.6 and 39.6 g/L across all three duplicated dilution rates... conversion of total sugars decreased gradual
growth_records.notes: DDAP-H run, D=0.02 h-1. Measured: SA titre 38.6-39.6 g/L (use 39.6 max), max yield 0.78 g/g total sugars, total-sugar conversion 81.3% at this D. DDAP-H feed (Table 2, as fed): glucose 8.3, xylose 52.6, galactose 3.7, arabinose 5.3 (total 69.9 g/L) plus acetic acid 0.9, furfural 0.62, HMF 0.11. Per-sugar conversion ranges across runs: glucose 94.3-97.5%, xylose 73.0-83.2%, arabinose 61.5-70.0%, galactose 37.9-47.9%; all consumed simultaneously (no CCR). Derived qSA = D x CSA = 0.02 x 39.6 = 0.79 g/L/h (consistent with reported lower bound 0.78). Derived total-sugar uptake = 0.02 x 69.9 x 0.813 = 1.14 g/L/h. CFA ~0. Furfural and HMF fully metabolised (remained at zero). Volumetric only; biomass negligible.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A+B) with DDAP-H corn stover hydrolysate, 70 g/L total sugars

up.method: approx: qSA/Y = 1.17/0.78; see notes (per-D conversion not individually tabulated)

sec.method: derived qSA = D x CSA = 0.03 x ~39

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor; mu decoupled from D)
growth_records.snippet: CSA remained fairly constant at between 38.6 and 39.6 g/L across all three duplicated dilution rates (0.02, 0.03, 0.04)... qSA increased linearly up to a D of 0.04 h-1
growth_records.notes: DDAP-H run, D=0.03 h-1. Measured SA titre in the 38.6-39.6 g/L band; yield ~0.78 g/g total sugars. Derived qSA = 0.03 x ~39.0 = 1.17 g/L/h (within reported 0.78-1.65 range for the three lower Ds). Total-sugar uptake estimated as qSA/yield = 1.17/0.78 = 1.50 g/L/h (marked non-experimental: exact conversion at 0.03 not individually reported; a value of 1.17 was entered conservatively — see qSA/yield estimate in text). CFA ~0. Volumetric; biomass negligible.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A+B) with DDAP-H corn stover hydrolysate, 70 g/L total sugars

up.method: approx = qSA/yield = 1.65/0.78; per-D conversion not individually tabulated

sec.method: reported upper qSA for the lower three dilution rates (qSA = D x CSA = 0.04 x ~39.6)

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor; mu decoupled from D)
growth_records.snippet: qSA increased linearly up to a D of 0.04 h-1 due to CSA remaining fairly constant... The SA productivity (qSA) was competitive and ranged between 0.78 and 1.65 g/L/h for the lower three dilution rates
growth_records.notes: DDAP-H run, D=0.04 h-1. Measured SA titre ~39.6 g/L (max in 38.6-39.6 band), yield ~0.78 g/g total sugars. qSA = D x CSA = 0.04 x 39.6 = 1.58-1.65 g/L/h (reported upper end 1.65). Total-sugar uptake estimated = qSA/yield = 1.65/0.78 = 2.12 g/L/h (non-experimental estimate; exact conversion at 0.04 not separately tabulated). CFA ~0. This D showed the most stable prolonged steady state (~5 volume turnovers). Volumetric; biomass negligible.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (parts A+B) with DDAP-H corn stover hydrolysate, 70 g/L total sugars

up.method: reported total sugars consumption rate at D=0.05 (volumetric)

sec.method: reported CFA near zero except CAA decreased with CSA at this D

growth_records.cite: Bradfield, Michael F A et al. Continuous succinic acid production by Actinobacillus succinogenes on xylose-enriched hydrolysate. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0363-3. [PMC4650334]
growth_records.method: reported (biofilm immobilised-cell reactor; mu decoupled from D)
growth_records.snippet: The highest qSA of 1.77 g/L/h was achieved at D=0.05 h-1 with a corresponding total sugars consumption rate of 2.56 g/L/h... yield decreased to 0.69 g/g at D=0.05; CSA decreased to 33.7 g/L
growth_records.notes: DDAP-H run, D=0.05 h-1 (highest-productivity steady state). Measured: qSA 1.77 g/L/h, total-sugar consumption rate 2.56 g/L/h (explicit), SA titre 33.7 g/L, SA yield 0.69 g/g total sugars. Note Ysa dropped at 0.05 (metabolic-flux shift; sugar consumption rose non-linearly). SA molar = 1.77/118.09 x 1000 = 15.0 mmol/L/h. Xylose conversion at this D was 73.0%. CFA ~0. Volumetric; biomass negligible (DCW ~0.19 g/L, cited).

growth_records.strain: A. succinogenes (natural producer, optimized CO2 conditions)
growth_records.medium: Glucose-based medium with optimized CO2 partial pressure (composition not stated in review)

up.method: co-substrate (1 mol CO2 per mol succinate); not quantified

growth_records.cite: Yujin Cao et al. Fermentative Succinate Production: An Emerging Technology to Replace the Traditional Petrochemical Processes. BioMed Research International (2013). DOI:10.1155/2013/723412. [PMC3874355]
growth_records.snippet: Under the optimized conditions, a succinate concentration of 51.6 g/L with a yield of 75.8% was reached for the producer A. succinogenes [60]
growth_records.notes: REVIEW-COMPILED, secondhand from text, cited from primary ref [60]. Succinate 51.6 g/L (endpoint) at 75.8% yield under CO2-optimized conditions. A. succinogenes = facultative anaerobe (Table 1). No growth rate/medium/T/pH in review.

sec.method: derived: 72 h endpoint titer 18.37 g/L / 72 h
derived: 72 h endpoint titer 0.59 g/L / 72 h

yields.theoretical_Ysucc_glucose_mass: 1.12 g succinate/g glucose (with CO2, cited ref [14])

growth_records.cite: Tix, Julian et al. Actinobacillus succinogenes in Bioelectrochemical Systems: Influence of Electric Potentials and Carbon Fabric Electrodes on Fermentation Performance. Microorganisms (2025). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms13081720. [PMC12388281]
growth_records.method: not reported (no biomass/OD time-course or CDW concentration given; only relative biofilm weight-gain % reported)
growth_records.snippet: The results of the control experiments conducted at 0 mV illustrate the typical product distribution... Succinate was the primary product, reaching a concentration of 18.37 +/- 0.55 g/L. In contrast, lactate reached only 0.59 +/- 0.12 g/L a
growth_records.notes: Batch electro-fermentation control (0 mV vs Ag/AgCl), 72 h endpoint (Figure 4A). Only endpoint titers reported; no time-resolved uptake/secretion rate and no biomass concentration, so specific (per-gDW) rates and growth rate not derivable. Volumetric productivity computed as titer/time: succinate 18.37 g/L / 72 h = 0.2551 g/L/h; lactate 0.59 g/L / 72 h = 0.0082 g/L/h (succinate MW 118.09 => 18.37 g/L = 155.6 mmol/L). Initial glucose 30 g/L; residual glucose for control not stated so glucose uptake not derivable. Product distribution at low potential: succinate ~70%, lactate ~2% (Figure 4B). Theoretical max yield 1.71 mol/mol (1.12 g/g) cited from ref [14]. Cited prior data NOT from this study: Park & Zeikus 1999 [18] consumed 47.33 mM glucose (no current) vs 60.44 mM (with current); succinate 51.27 mM vs 82.88 mM.

sec.method: derived: 72 h endpoint titer 19.76 g/L / 72 h

growth_records.cite: Tix, Julian et al. Actinobacillus succinogenes in Bioelectrochemical Systems: Influence of Electric Potentials and Carbon Fabric Electrodes on Fermentation Performance. Microorganisms (2025). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms13081720. [PMC12388281]
growth_records.snippet: Among the tested potentials, -500 mV and -1000 mV yielded the highest succinate titers, with -500 mV standing out at 19.76 +/- 0.41 g/L. This observation aligns with previous studies reporting a positive impact of potentials between -600 an
growth_records.notes: Batch electro-fermentation at -500 mV vs Ag/AgCl (Figure 4A), 72 h endpoint, single-chamber BES. Volumetric succinate productivity = 19.76 g/L / 72 h = 0.2744 g/L/h. Only endpoint titer available; no residual glucose or biomass, so uptake and specific rates not derivable. -1000 mV also cited as among highest titers but no exact value given, so no separate record.

up.method: derived: (30 g/L initial - 15.62 g/L residual) = 14.38 g/L consumed / 72 h

sec.method: derived: 72 h endpoint titer 14.1 g/L / 72 h

growth_records.cite: Tix, Julian et al. Actinobacillus succinogenes in Bioelectrochemical Systems: Influence of Electric Potentials and Carbon Fabric Electrodes on Fermentation Performance. Microorganisms (2025). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms13081720. [PMC12388281]
growth_records.method: not reported (biomass qualitatively decreased at this potential, esp. on working electrode; no CDW value)
growth_records.snippet: At -2000 mV, the overall metabolic activity is inhibited, as indicated by a residual glucose concentration of 15.62 +/- 0.74 g/L after 72 h... succinate decreased from 19.76 +/- 0.41 g/L to 14.1 +/- 1.6 g/L... the final acetate titer at -20
growth_records.notes: Batch electro-fermentation at -2000 mV vs Ag/AgCl (Figure 4A + abstract), 72 h endpoint. Residual glucose 15.62 g/L reported => glucose consumed = 30 - 15.62 = 14.38 g/L; volumetric glucose uptake = 14.38/72 = 0.1997 g/L/h. Succinate titer 14.1 g/L (abstract) => 14.1/72 = 0.1958 g/L/h; derived succinate yield = 14.1/14.38 = 0.981 g/g. Acetate rose by 1.48 g/L vs control but control acetate absolute titer not given numerically, so acetate secretion rate not recorded. Metabolic inhibition and biomass decrease noted at this potential.
